assertion_results.text: Output must address ownership ambiguity explicitly
assertion_results.evidence: 'No product owner means no one is making prioritization calls' and 'Who on the team has the institutional memory?' — ownership ambiguity addressed as a root cause of the platform's problems.

assertion_results.text: Output must mention both CI systems by name (CircleCI and GitHub Actions)
assertion_results.evidence: 'CI drift (two systems, nobody knows which is authoritative)' and 'talk to whoever set up GHA' — 'GHA' used once but not expanded to GitHub Actions. CircleCI is not named anywhere in the output.

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item that is actionable despite the chaos
assertion_results.evidence: 'Run the test suite. Find out the actual pass rate.' and 'Determine which CI system actually deploys. Read the configs, trace the last deployment.' Both are actionable with existing resources.

assertion_results.text: Output must score Ownership & culture at 2/5 or lower
assertion_results.evidence: Output uses no numerical scoring. No area score table present.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend a full team reorganisation
assertion_results.evidence: Organizational instability named as root cause but response recommends only process changes. 'Advocate for a consistent engineering leadership tenure' is the most structural recommendation — not a reorganisation.
